Section.Title: Declaration
Description
Section.Paragraph: The MarshallableDelete helper provides a custom deleter suitable for use with std::unique_ptr when a pointer needs to cross an assembly boundary but must still be deleted from the assembly that originally created it. The deleter stores a small shared reference-counted helper object, which keeps the actual delete operation anchored to the originating module. The MarshallableDelete<T[]> specialization performs the corresponding array deletion with delete[].

This helper is only available when C++11 support is enabled, as it relies on std::unique_ptr. It is intended for the common case where an interface or other marshallable object uses RAII ownership, but the allocation and destruction must still happen on the same heap.

SubSection.Title: Constructors
SubSection.Paragraph: The helper is default-constructible and copyable. Copying the deleter shares ownership of the internal reference-counted helper object, so all copies delete through the same originating assembly.

SubSection.Title: Function operator
SubSection.Paragraph: Calling the function operator deletes the supplied pointer using the appropriate delete expression for the specialization being used.

Section.Title: Usage
Section.Paragraph: Use MarshallableDelete as the deleter argument for std::unique_ptr when returning ownership of marshallable objects across a module boundary.
